sources.name: Badlands (Borzou Daragahi)
sources.focus: Daily intelligence on global affairs, Lebanon crisis, Iran war dynamics. Veteran Middle East correspondent.

sources.name: Mick Ryan — The Learning Curve
sources.focus: Military learning across Ukraine, Middle East, Indo-Pacific. PLA study of Iran War, CENTCOM Hormuz operations.

sources.name: Marc Schulman — Tel Aviv Diary
sources.focus: Daily diaries: Iran-Hormuz dynamics, Israel-Lebanon ceasefire violations, Hezbollah fiber-optic drone warfare.

sources.name: Peter Zeihan on Geopolitics
sources.focus: Data-driven forecasts on Hormuz as Asian fuel artery, oil price shock modeling ($20-50/bbl). 500k+ subscribers.

sources.name: Maritime Executive Daily
sources.focus: Real-time shipping risks, insurance hikes, vessel rerouting, LNG trade exposure (93% Qatar LNG at risk).

sources.name: Iran Observer (Amir Taheri)
sources.focus: Tehran politics, IRGC maneuvers, Hezbollah rearmament via Iranian smuggling routes. Hardliner consolidation post-ceasefire.

sources.name: Gulf Analysis
sources.focus: Iran proxies, Hormuz diplomacy, Gulf energy security. Authored by ex-diplomats.

sources.name: The Levant Report (Hanin Ghaddar)
sources.focus: Lebanon-Hezbollah nexus with Iranian funding, Beirut reporting, IRGC grip on Lebanese ports.

sources.name: Middle East Contrarian (Jonathan Spyer)
sources.focus: Syria-Lebanon-Iran triangle, field dispatches from Lebanon, skeptical of Western proxy narratives.

sources.name: World Brief Daily
sources.focus: Daily geopolitical briefings with geospatial OSINT: Iranian cave-hidden anti-ship missiles, US destroyer deployments, sea mine threats.

sources.name: OSINT 613 (@OSINT_613)
sources.focus: Iran war OSINT aggregator: Russian drone shipments to Bandar Anzali, IDF maritime strikes, PRC satellite imagery support to IRGC. 245k followers.

sources.name: Aurora Intel (@AuroraIntel)
sources.focus: Live AIS tracking of Iranian oil tankers evading Hormuz post-Kharg spill, Russian Caspian drone convoys. 180k followers.

sources.name: MT Anderson (@MT_Anderson)
sources.focus: Qatar-mediated tanker routes, Chinese satellite firms aiding IRGC strikes, fiber-optic drone shipments. 95k followers.

sources.name: Intel Crab (@IntelCrab)
sources.focus: Russian Starlink-equipped drone shipments, oil spill impact on LNG carriers, sanctioned Chinese imagery. 340k followers.

sources.name: Alma Research and Education Center
sources.focus: Intelligence on Hezbollah, Lebanon, Iran. Post-ceasefire airstrike tracking (299 strikes Apr 18-May 3), forward defense lines, professional casualty analysis.

sources.name: Flashpoint Blog — Operation Epic Fury
sources.focus: Real-time tracking of US-Israel strikes on Iran: cyber threats, infrastructure hits, proxy responses.

sources.name: Windward.ai Maritime OSINT
sources.focus: SAR/EO satellite imagery for dark fleet tracking (84% dark vessels near Hormuz), GPS jamming (470 incidents/day), Kharg Island covert VLCC loading.

sources.name: Kpler Defense Intelligence
sources.focus: Real-time AIS + satellite for 400k+ vessels; dark port calls, AIS spoofing, STS transfers, sanctions evasion alerts. 5-second latency.

sources.name: ShipFinder Hormuz Live
sources.focus: Live Persian Gulf traffic, Hormuz in/out transits, vessel data (MMSI, flag, DWT), crude oil prices.

sources.name: France Charles de Gaulle CSG — Hormuz Guardian Force
sources.focus: European guardian force for Strait of Hormuz: French carrier group + Italian/Dutch escorts, Franco-British plan for post-conflict escort ops.

sources.name: IranWire
sources.focus: Crowd-sourced Persian/English blog: hardliner purges, Hezbollah arms flows through Iraqi corridors. Fully independent.

sources.name: Hussain Abdul-Hussain
sources.focus: Trump 'Deal of the Century' for Lebanon: Hezbollah disarmament, Israeli withdrawal, border demarcation, reconstruction framework.

sources.name: Global Geopolitics (globalgeopolitics.co.uk)
sources.focus: US maritime deterrence doctrine, Hormuz chokepoint attrition analysis, multipolar consequences. Destroyer withdrawal under missile pressure, inversions of US maritime supremacy doctrine.

sources.name: Applied Geopolitics (appliedgeopolitics.com)
sources.focus: Data-driven global oil disruption analysis: Energy Shock and Global Oil Market Disruption (May 11). Brent 14/bbl, 14.5M bpd offline. Uses Bloomberg, EIA, shipping index data.

sources.name: Responsible Statecraft (Quincy Institute)
sources.focus: Critical analysis of US-Iran war, Hormuz as economic siege, limits of American primacy, Iran three-phase peace framework.

sources.name: FFUS Substack (Louis-Vincent Gave / Gavekal Research)
sources.focus: New Global Order analysis: Iran war structural implications, US Navy assumption breakdown, AI/China macro. Gavekal premier Hong Kong macro research house.

sources.name: Loose Cannon (loosecannon.substack.com)
sources.focus: Navigational realities of Hormuz: US inshore southern channel vs Iranian toll route vs mined central channel. Former Navy SEAL/boatbuilder.

sources.name: Michael D. Sellers (michaeldsellers.substack.com)
sources.focus: CIA assessment on Iran blockade endurance (90-120 day survival), 75% missile launcher survival rate, blockade-as-endurance-test framework.

sources.name: Jin Low (jinlow.substack.com)
sources.focus: China energy exposure to Hormuz closure: 45-50% crude imports via Hormuz, land route buffer capacity analysis.

sources.name: Spatial Intelligence (Bilawal Sidhu)
sources.focus: Inside Palantir Maven Smart System: AI fusion for Iran war targeting, Kharg Island March 13 ops, God Eye View OSINT platform.

sources.name: Middle East Council (mecouncil.org)
sources.focus: Iran War and Emerging Geopolitical Order: multipolarity analysis, supply chain security, semiconductor/digital infrastructure as new security domains.

sources.name: Moon of Alabama (moonofalabama.org)
sources.focus: Alternative geopolitical analysis: Iran war on ground narratives, Project Freedom failure critique. Treat as contrarian perspective; cross-reference with ISW/CTP.

sources.name: Dr. Jennifer Considine (drjennifericonsidine.substack.com)
sources.focus: Energy politics and oil market updates, Hormuz disruption market impacts, Brent price analysis.

local_language_sources.name: Mehr News Agency (English)
local_language_sources.focus: Iran domestic politics, IRGC statements, regional diplomacy, Hormuz crisis coverage. Semi-official Iranian news agency.
local_language_sources.note: Semi-official. Content bias toward IRGC/national security framing. Valuable for Iranian official narrative baseline.

local_language_sources.name: Tasnim News Agency (English)
local_language_sources.focus: IRGC-affiliated news. Iran military operations, nuclear negotiations, proxy force activity. Official IRGC-aligned perspective.
local_language_sources.note: IRGC-aligned. Critical for understanding Iranian military narrative. Counterbalance to Western sources.

local_language_sources.name: Al Arabiya English
local_language_sources.focus: Middle East news from Saudi/GCC perspective. Hormuz crisis, Gulf security, energy markets.

local_language_sources.name: Al Arabiya Arabic
local_language_sources.focus: Arabic-language coverage of Middle East, GCC policy, Hormuz crisis, Iran regional activity.
local_language_sources.note: Arabic original. Useful for detecting narrative differences between Arabic and English editions.

local_language_sources.name: Asharq Al-Awsat English
local_language_sources.focus: Pan-Arab daily covering Middle East politics, energy, Gulf security. Saudi-owned London-based.

local_language_sources.name: Asharq Al-Awsat Arabic
local_language_sources.focus: Arabic edition of leading pan-Arab daily. Deeper regional analysis and opinion.
local_language_sources.note: Arabic original edition. Often contains analysis not syndicated to English edition.

local_language_sources.name: TASS Russian News Agency (English)
local_language_sources.focus: Official Russian state news agency. Ukraine war, Russia-Iran relations, energy geopolitics, BRICS narratives.
local_language_sources.note: Official Russian state media. Essential for Kremlin narrative tracking.

local_language_sources.name: Meduza (English)
local_language_sources.focus: Independent Russian news from exile. Ukraine war, Kremlin politics, Russian society, repression tracking.
local_language_sources.note: Blocked in Russia. Valuable for independent/opposition Russian perspective.

local_language_sources.name: Meduza (Russian)
local_language_sources.focus: Russian-language independent news coverage not syndicated to English edition.
local_language_sources.note: Russian-language feed. Contains original reporting not available in English edition.

local_language_sources.name: The Moscow Times
local_language_sources.focus: English-language independent Russian news. Business, politics, sanctions, Kremlin insider reporting.
local_language_sources.note: Dutch-registered independent outlet. Post-crackdown diaspora journalism.

local_language_sources.name: Xinhua News Agency (English)
local_language_sources.focus: Official Chinese state news agency. China foreign policy, BRI, global diplomacy, Iran-China relations, energy security.
local_language_sources.note: Official PRC state media. Essential for Chinese government narrative tracking.

local_language_sources.name: Global Times
local_language_sources.focus: Nationalistic Chinese perspective on foreign affairs, US-China tensions, Taiwan, Iran crisis, global order.
local_language_sources.note: English edition of party-nationalist tabloid. Combative foreign policy narrative. Valuable for hawkish PRC signaling.

local_language_sources.name: Haaretz (English)
local_language_sources.focus: Left-leaning Israeli newspaper. Israel-Iran war coverage, military assessment, regional diplomacy, intelligence community leaks.
local_language_sources.note: Israel's newspaper of record. Left-leaning editorial stance, but strong defense/intelligence reporting.

local_language_sources.name: Ynet News (Hebrew)
local_language_sources.focus: Major Hebrew-language Israeli news site. Military operations, security incidents, breaking news.
local_language_sources.note: Hebrew original. Breaking news often appears here before English-language outlets.

local_language_sources.name: Times of Israel
local_language_sources.focus: English-language Israeli news covering Iran conflict, military operations, diplomacy, casualty reporting.

local_language_sources.name: CGTN (China Global TV Network)
local_language_sources.focus: Chinese state broadcaster English edition. Global news from PRC perspective.

local_language_sources.name: Israel Hayom
local_language_sources.focus: Right-leaning free Hebrew daily. Netanyahu-friendly, security-focused reporting.
local_language_sources.note: Right-leaning Hebrew source. Useful for tracking Netanyahu government narrative.

local_language_sources.name: El País English
local_language_sources.focus: Leading Spanish newspaper English edition. EU foreign policy, Latin America, European energy crisis.

local_language_sources.name: Le Monde (English)
local_language_sources.focus: Leading French newspaper English edition. EU defense, NATO, Middle East policy.

local_language_sources.name: Kommersant (English)
local_language_sources.focus: Business-oriented Russian independent newspaper. Russian economic outlook, sanctions impact, business climate.
local_language_sources.note: Relatively independent Russian business daily. Useful for economic sentiment tracking.

local_language_sources.name: Nikkei Asia
local_language_sources.focus: Japan's leading financial newspaper Asia edition. Asian energy security, China tensions, supply chains.
